twistCard: Word travels fast in SF. By the time you make it to Caltrain, three Twitter accounts already know what you said in the room.

scenes.title: Scene 1 · The Pivot
scenes.imagePrompt: co-working space at dusk, female cofounder mid-sentence leaning across a laptop covered in stickers, the founder visible in profile listening, golden hour glow, two-shot composition, urgent mood

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: We need to talk before the pitch. I've been thinking — Wagr needs to pivot to AI.

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: Everyone on Sand Hill is asking about AI agents. Peer-to-peer betting is a tough sell right now.

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: We wrap Wagr in an AI layer — 'your AI friend who handles all your bets' — and suddenly we're a platform play.

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: I can have a demo ready by Thursday. But I need you to back me on this. Right now.

choices.label: No, ship the original
choices.consequence: Founder holds the line; cofounder reads it as a no-confidence vote.

choices.label: Yes, pivot to AI
choices.consequence: Founder folds; the deck gets a new title slide before midnight.

scenes.title: Scene 2 · The Scoop
scenes.imagePrompt: a SoMa coffee shop with exposed brick, a male tech reporter holding a notebook, smirking, leaning forward across a small table, the founder's hands wrapped around a coffee cup in foreground, slight noir lighting, two-shot

dialogue.speaker: reporter
dialogue.text: I heard from three sources that Wagr's payment processor dropped you over regulatory concerns. Care to comment?

dialogue.speaker: reporter
dialogue.text: I'm running this either way. The question is whether your version of the story is in it.

dialogue.speaker: reporter
dialogue.text: Off the record — I actually think what you're building is interesting. But my editor wants blood.

dialogue.speaker: reporter
dialogue.text: Give me something. Anything. A leak, a number, a name. I'll make it worth your while.

choices.label: That's not true. No comment.
choices.consequence: Founder denies; the story runs anyway, harsher.

choices.label: Send her the details
choices.consequence: Founder leaks; the cofounder finds out from Twitter.

scenes.title: Scene 3 · The Term Sheet
scenes.imagePrompt: the bar at Rosewood Sand Hill at last light, a patrician male VC in a perfectly tailored blazer across a small marble table, founder's hands clasped, a leather portfolio between them, cinematic two-shot, deal-closing tension

dialogue.speaker: vc
dialogue.text: Tell me something that's true that almost nobody agrees with you on.

dialogue.speaker: vc
dialogue.text: We want to lead your seed. Three million, fifteen percent, standard pro-rata.

dialogue.speaker: vc
dialogue.text: One condition. Maya steps back to an advisory role. We want you running point, solo.

dialogue.speaker: vc
dialogue.text: This offer expires when I leave this table. The Caltrain back to the city is in eleven minutes.

choices.label: Take it
choices.consequence: Founder accepts; the cofounder isn't told tonight.

choices.label: Walk away
choices.consequence: Founder declines on principle; the term sheet goes to a competitor by Friday.

scenes.title: Scene 4 · Trust Crisis
scenes.imagePrompt: founder's apartment kitchen at midnight, female cofounder standing across the counter holding a phone, screen glow lighting her face, the founder seated, two-shot, raw emotional confrontation, harsh overhead lighting

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: I saw the term sheet. The one with my name in the advisory clause.

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: You were going to tell me when, exactly?

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: I built the entire backend. I have thirty percent. Whatever you're about to say — think carefully.

choices.label: Lie. You never saw it.
choices.consequence: Founder denies; the cofounder pretends to believe it for one more week.

choices.label: Level with her
choices.consequence: Founder confesses; one of them is leaving by morning.
